name: CMMS
version_status: CONFIRMED for Atlas CMMS target / app identity is INFERRED
what: Computerized maintenance management system for assets, preventive maintenance, work orders, and service records.
solves: Maintenance planning, asset service schedules, inspections, spare parts, and technician workflows.
users: Operations, facilities, service, and maintenance teams.
fit: Useful if Hylono manages serviceable devices, refurbishment cycles, and field maintenance across rental inventory. Stronger fit as an operations tool than as a customer-facing system.
runtime: Web application with maintenance workflows, database, user roles, and operational dashboards.
components: Work orders, assets, schedules, inspections, parts, reports, and admin/users.
deploy: Self-host via official docs or repo guidance; production needs database, mail, backups, and operations ownership.
deps: Database, mail, storage, scheduler/background jobs, and operational process definition.
auth: Local users/roles; verify current SSO options if required.
apis: APIs and integrations should be validated against current Atlas CMMS docs before implementation assumptions.
security: Operational asset data is not the highest compliance burden, but service histories and assigned personnel data still require access control.
setup_path: Treat app identity as inferred and re-verify before implementation. If Atlas CMMS is correct, start from official docs and self-host guidance.
env: DB settings, app URL, mail, storage, scheduler config, and secrets.
persistence: Back up maintenance DB and any documents/images linked to work orders.
operations: Preventive-maintenance cadence, data hygiene for asset/service histories, and workflow ownership matter more than raw infrastructure complexity.
failure_points: Using a CMMS before asset/service taxonomy is defined, or overlapping it badly with Leihs/Snipe-IT/Fleetbase.
integration: Useful alongside Leihs for rental fleet maintenance, with n8n for service alerts and Novu for operational notifications.
verify: Primary unresolved item: confirm that 'CMMS' in your inventory really means Atlas CMMS / Grashjs.
risk: Moderate due to identity ambiguity and process-design dependency.
gdpr: Mainly operational data, but technician/user assignments can still be personal data.
overlap: Overlaps partly with Leihs asset records and Fleetbase operations, but is strongest specifically for maintenance.
codex_read_first: Verify the intended CMMS product identity before doing implementation work.
codex_best_path: If Atlas CMMS is correct, position it as maintenance back office only, integrated to rental/service events rather than exposed publicly.
codex_never_assume: Never assume the target product name. Never assume asset-rental, maintenance, and fleet logistics belong in one system.

assets.name: Official repo
assets.notes: Atlas CMMS primary code source if identity is correct.

assets.notes: Self-host/user guidance if Atlas CMMS is the intended app.
